channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Dozens of survivors and dead pulled from abandoned South African mine as hundreds remain underground
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa (AP) — Months after South African authorities initially cut off supplies to miners working illegally in an abandoned gold mine, rescuers brought dozens of bodies and emaciated survivors to the surface Tuesday with hundreds more still believed to be underground, many of them
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Russia's top diplomat praises Trump's views on Ukraine conflict
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s top diplomat said Tuesday that Moscow is open for talks with President-elect Donald Trump and praised him for pointing to NATO's plan to embrace Ukraine as a root cause of the nearly 3-year-old conflict.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: U.S. food inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the Jarratt, Virginia, factory that was shut down last year.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: No. 2 Senate Democratic leader will back Rubio for secretary of State
item.description: Senate Democratic Whip Dick Durbin (Ill.) on Monday announced that he will vote for Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to become the next secretary of State, a sign that Rubio is on a path to be confirmed on President-elect Trump’s first day of office with overwhelming support.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson backs conditions on California fire aid
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is backing placing conditions on disaster assistance for California in the wake of the destructive wildfires blazing around the Los Angeles area, a position already drawing opposition from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Machete-wielding suspect at US Capitol granted pre-trial release days before Trump inauguration
item.description: The man accused of trying to smuggle a machete into the US Capitol has a violent history that prompted his parents to ask a court to get him out of their home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rashida Jones is stepping down as MSNBC president on the eve of Trump inauguration
item.description: MSNBC President Rashida Jones says she is stepping down after four years leading the liberal news network, her move coming on the eve of a second Trump administration and after changes in corporate ownership.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden health officials say they built up US pandemic defenses. Trump promises changes
item.description: The Biden administration is releasing a “roadmap” for maintaining government defenses against infectious diseases, just as President-elect Donald Trump pledges to dismantle some of them.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Blue city judge slammed for releasing violent suspect arrested again for vicious attack on teen
item.description: Seattle Judge Veronica Galvan released an armed robbery suspect who then allegedly viciously attacked a 14-year-old in what critics say is a soft-on-crime issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wildfires latest: Firefighters prepare for dry conditions and strong winds
item.description: Firefighters around Los Angeles were preparing on Tuesday to attack flare-ups or new blazes. The National Weather Service issued a rare warning that dry winds combined with severely dry conditions created a “ particularly dangerous situation," indicating that any new fire could explode in size.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge says the New Orleans Police Department can begin the process of ending federal oversight
item.description: A judge says the New Orleans Police Department can begin the process of ending longstanding federal oversight.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sheriff calls on wildfire evacuees to be patient amid 'grim' search for bodies
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna asked community members to be patient with local officials, as residents eagerly await permission to return to their homes in southern California, where tens of thousands of acres have been decimated by wildfires in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's Cabinet pick confirmation hearings set to begin and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Multiracial boom in 2020 census was mostly an illusion, researchers say
item.description: When the 2020 census results were released, they showed a boom in the number of people classified as multiracial in the United States since 2010. Two Princeton sociologists now say that jump was mostly an illusion.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden says he's leaving Trump with a 'strong hand to play' in world conflicts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Monday that his stewardship of American foreign policy has left the U.S. safer and economically more secure, arguing that President-elect Donald Trump will inherit a nation viewed as stronger and more reliable than it was four years ago.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carrie Underwood to perform at Trump inauguration
item.description: Country music star Carrie Underwood is set to take the stage on Inauguration Day, a sign that President-elect Trump's second inauguration may be drawing more high-profile talent than his first go round.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Beyoncé donates $2.5 million to fire relief and Meghan delays show launch due to the disaster
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Weaker winds and intense firefighting efforts over the weekend boosted containment of fires burning in and around Los Angeles, but the effects of the blazes on the entertainment world continue.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: List: The 'overused' words banished for 2025 by a Michigan university
item.description: Sorry not sorry, but one Michigan university would appreciate if you did not utilize these words, period.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, Fox 5

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fired Disney World employee pleads guilty to hacking menu, removing life-saving allergy information
item.description: A former Disney World employee changed menus to falsely indicate that food items didn't contain deadly allergens like peanuts, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: California police arrest multiple drone pilots as firefighters battle infernos
item.description: Los Angeles police arrested three people following drone incidents in wildfire areas as first responders battle infernos raging in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Photos: Handful of L.A. homes remain untouched amid charred neighborhoods
item.description: The Walsh House, featured in the “Beverly Hills 90210" television series, survived. So did its pristine lawn. Next door, the remains of a neighbor's home was blackened with soot.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: PHOTOS: West Virginia father-son duo build ice dragon igloo
item.description: While many of us were staying cozy indoors, a father-son duo was hard at work making the best of this week's wintery weather by building a one-of-a-kind igloo.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gorski, ABC 4

item.title: Lamar Jackson and Josh Allen will face off for a berth in the AFC championship game
item.description: Lamar Jackson vs. Josh Allen won’t decide the NFL MVP next week. The stakes are higher with a spot in the AFC championship game on the line.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 24 dead as Los Angeles fires leave destruction, devastation in wake; strong winds expected this week
item.description: Firefighters scrambled Sunday to make further progress against wildfires that have destroyed thousands of homes and killed 24 people in the Los Angeles area as forecasters again warned of dangerous weather with the return of strong winds this week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fires burn Los Angeles schools and destroy outdoor education sanctuaries
item.description: For Irina Contreras, a program manager for Los Angeles County’s Department of Arts and Culture, outdoor education was a refuge for both her and her daughter during the pandemic. Now, much of that refuge has been burned in the raging wildfires around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY and SHARON LURYE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Southern areas hit by winter storm thaw and power slowly returns
item.description: After a freezing winter storm shut schools, cut power and cancelled or delayed flights, the South was slowly thawing Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: “It’s horrible when you realize that you’re the one that actually gave them the milk that killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: 16 dead, more than a dozen missing as crews continue fighting blazes
item.description: More than a dozen people have died as a result of the still-spreading wildfires burning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Kohl's to close 27 stores by April as struggling department stores works to improve sales
item.description: Kohl’s said Friday it was closing 27 underperforming locations in 15 states by April — a fraction of its 1,150 store base — as the struggling department store chain aims to boost profitability and improve sagging sales.
item.dc:creator: Anne D'Innocenzio, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: A California man whose two cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu risk says he meant to keep his beloved pets healthy, but his efforts tragically backfired.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires death toll rises as crews fight heavy winds to save homes and landmarks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The death toll from the wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area rose to 16 as crews battled to cut off the spreading blazes before potentially strong winds return that could push the flames toward some of the city’s most famous landmarks.
item.dc:creator: JAE HONG, HOLLY RAMER and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Texans get 2 points on blocked extra point return vs. Chargers, 1st in NFL playoff history
item.description: Houston's D'Angelo Ross returned a blocked extra point for two points in the fourth quarter of the Texans' wild-card playoff victory over the Los Angeles Chargers on Saturday — the first such play in NFL postseason history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden gives Pope Francis highest civilian honor before leaving office
item.description: Biden, who is only the second Catholic president in U.S. history, spoke with the Pope by phone on Saturday to tell him he was a recipient of the medal, which is the highest civilian honor.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walmart recalls 12,000 cartons of chicken broth
item.description: Walmart has recalled over 12,000 cartons of Great Value Family Size Chicken Broth due to packaging issues that could lead to spoilage, according to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, WFLA, NBC
